zh.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire是Alexander Meldrum Blythe, I的兒子，於1435年出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可朗 斯特拉頓。他於1458年與Isabel Jermyn在英國英格蘭諾福爾可登斯頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1490年在英國英格蘭諾福爾可朗 斯特拉頓享年55歲。

nameMeanings.name: Blythe,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Anglia): from Middle English blithe ‘joyful, happy’ (Old English blīthe ‘gentle, merry’) or from an unrecorded personal name, Old English Blītha, derived from this word.
Scottish: habitational name from Blythe in Lauderdale, Berwickshire, and possibly also from Blyth in Peeblesshire.

English: habitational name from Blyth in Northumberland or Nottinghamshire, or Blythe in Warwickshire, named for the rivers on which they stand. The river name is from Old English blīthe ‘gentle, pleasant’.

variants.name: Bligh
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Blythe , with Anglo-Norman loss of th.
Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Ó Blighe ‘descendant of Blighe’, probably a Gaelicized form of the Old Norse byname Blígr (from blígja ‘to gaze’).
Cornish: nickname from Cornish blyth ‘wolf’. Compare Blethen .

variants.name: Blyth
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Blythe  .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire was born in 1435, in Long Stratton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom as the son of Alexander Meldrum Blythe, I. He married Isabel Jermyn in 1458, in Dunston, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1490, in Long Stratton, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire est né en 1435, à Long Stratton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Alexander Meldrum Blythe, I. Il a épousé Isabel Jermyn en 1458, à Dunston, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1490, à Long Stratton, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquireは1435年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクロング ストラットオンでAlexander Meldrum Blythe, Iの息子として生まれた。1458年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクダンストンでIsabel Jermynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1490年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクロング ストラットオンで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire(은)는 1435년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 론그 스트래턴에서 Alexander Meldrum Blythe, I의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1458년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 던스턴에서 Isabel Jermyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 론그 스트래턴에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander Blythe, II II, Esquire родился в 1435 г. в местности лонг страттон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Alexander Meldrum Blythe, I. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Jermyn в 1458 г. в местности дунстон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1490 г. в местности лонг страттон, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 55 лет.
